title: epipactis palustris f. ericetorum
intro: Epipactis palustris f. ericetorum, also known as marsh helleborine, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to take advantage of its medicinal properties, but more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
